articles.title: Cory Sandhagen on Sean O'Malley callout, Aljo & Merab losses, pursuit of UFC title | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: DC is BACK with another edition of the Daniel Cormier Show, this time featuring one of the most talented bantamweights in the UFC today, Cory Sandhagen. Cory talks about his upbringing in Colorado, and switching from basketball to fighting in his later teenage years. DC and Sandhagen also go back and look at his UFC career and the tough losses he had to take to Aljamain Sterling and Merab Dvalishvili in hopes to obtain a UFC championship. Plus, Cory dives into his callout of Sean O'Malley and why he feels he is deserving of that White House fight against Suga. Plus, check out Sandhagen talk about his journey to the UFC, and how his mental approach to the sport is so different than most people in the fight game. All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: Jake Paul Is Playing a Dangerous Game
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to Jake Paul claiming that Francis Ngannou can’t box—and breaks down why that statement doesn’t add up. After watching Ngannou push Tyson Fury to the limit and nearly walk away with the heavyweight title, Chael questions the narrative while also giving Jake full credit for proving his toughness against names like Nate Diaz and Anderson Silva. But does Jake really need to take on Ngannou, or has he already proven everything he set out to prove? Chael dives into the real purpose behind these fights—whether it’s money, attention, or something deeper—and why chasing danger for its own sake may not be the right move. #JakePaul #FrancisNgannou #Boxing #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Jake Paul Calls Out Ngannou 0:30 “Ngannou Can’t Box?” 1:00 Jake’s Evolution as a Fighter 1:40 From Doubter to Believer 2:10 Jake’s Toughness Proven 2:50 Why This Fight Isn’t Necessary 3:20 Ngannou vs Tyson Fury Reality 4:00 The Olympic Question 5:00 Could Jake Compete at That Level? 6:00 What Are These Fights Really For? 7:00 Courage vs Risk 8:00 Final Take: Jake Has Proven Enough Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Real Reason Conor vs Chandler Isn’t Happening
articles.description: Chael Sonnen is confident that Conor McGregor is officially on his way back—but not in the way fans expected. After direct conversations and behind-the-scenes movement, Chael explains why the long-awaited fight with Michael Chandler is no longer the right matchup and what the UFC’s real plan is for Conor’s return. From the impact of his injury to the importance of rebuilding momentum, Chael lays out how this comeback will be structured and why Conor may be thrown directly into high-level competition against top contenders like Ian Machado Garry or others in the division. This isn’t about hype—it’s about proving he still belongs. The real question isn’t if Conor returns… it’s what happens when he does. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MichaelChandler #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Conor McGregor Is Coming Back 0:30 Why Chael Is Certain 1:00 Why Chandler Fight Is Dead 1:40 UFC’s Brand Promise Explained 2:20 Why They Have to Restart 3:00 Why Chandler No Longer Fits 3:40 Who Conor Could Fight Instead 4:20 The Anderson Silva Comparison 5:10 How the UFC Will Use Conor 5:50 Conor Is Willing to Fight Anyone 6:30 The Weight Class Surprise 7:00 Why This Comeback Is Different 7:40 The Real Stakes of This Fight 8:10 One Fight Decides Everything Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Netflix Isn’t Beating the UFC, It’s Proving Them Right
articles.description: Netflix Isn’t Beating the UFC, It’s Proving Them Right Chael Sonnen breaks down why the idea that Netflix can compete with the UFC is completely misguided. While big names like Ronda Rousey, Francis Ngannou, and Nate Diaz bring attention, Chael explains why stacking former stars doesn’t build a real promotion. From sold-out UFC arenas without announced main events to the deeper reality of roster building, rankings, and star creation, this is a sharp look at why the UFC remains untouchable. Chael makes the case that Netflix isn’t threatening the UFC—it’s reinforcing exactly why the UFC model works. For more analysis and inside perspective, stay locked in. #UFC #MMA #Netflix #DanaWhite #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Netflix Is Proving the UFC Right 0:20 Why Fans Don’t Know What They Want 0:50 The “Best Fighters” Argument 1:20 Big Names vs Real Competition 1:50 Why This Isn’t a Real Promotion 2:10 Netflix Is Buying Memories 2:40 The Ronda Rousey Angle 3:10 Fighter Pay vs Real Threat 3:40 The Perth Sellout Example 4:10 The Difference Between UFC and Everyone Else 4:40 Why You Can’t Beat the UFC 5:10 The Real Problem: Building Stars 5:40 Gaethje & Chandler Example 6:10 The Power of “UFC” 6:40 Final Reality Check Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Tom Aspinall Is Making a Huge Mistake
articles.description: Get $10 Off at BRUNT with code CHAEL at https://www.bruntworkwear.com/CHAEL #BruntpodChael Sonnen breaks down why he’s genuinely concerned about Tom Aspinall and the direction his career is taking. From shifting focus toward contracts and money to sounding more like Jon Jones than the hungry contender he once was, Chael explains why this change could be dangerous. He lays out Aspinall’s real options—return to pure competition, stay disciplined, or risk losing the very mindset that made him special—and why chasing financial narratives instead of fights can derail even the best. This is a deeper look at what it really takes to stay champion in the UFC, and why the mental shift might matter more than any opponent. articles.title: The Team Race Myth in College Wrestling #ufc
articles.description: Let’s tell the truth nobody wants to say: Wrestlers don’t care about the team race. Not because they’re selfish. Not because they’re bad teammates. Because wrestling is the ultimate individual sport. When you step on that mat, it’s just you. Your lungs. Your weight cut. Your mistakes. Your moment. Scoreboards don’t shoot takedowns. Team points don’t fight off your back. The “team title” conversation? That’s for coaching staffs, media desks, and broadcast graphics. Ask the athlete cutting weight at 6 AM. Ask the guy icing his knees alone in the hallway. Ask the wrestler staring at the bracket with one name circled. He’s chasing a national title. His title. And dominance doesn’t ruin the sport. Programs like Penn State Nittany Lions wrestling don’t hurt wrestling — they raise the standard. Dynasties don’t make competition worse. They force everyone else to level up. Better rooms. Better systems. Better athletes. Greatness creates pressure. Pressure creates growth. The sport doesn’t suffer when one program wins. The sport evolves. Because wrestling has always been simple: One mat. One match. One man’s will. Team trophies are celebrated. Individual titles are remembered. And every wrestler knows the difference. Win your match. Score your points. Earn your legacy. Everything else is noise. #Wrestling #CollegeWrestling #NCAAWrestling #PennStateWrestling #CombatSports #Grappling #MatLife #WrestlerMindset #IndividualSport

articles.title: The Legend of Chuck Norris #ufc
articles.description: “Feel bad for the Grim Reaper having to face Chuck Norris.” That joke exists for a reason. Chuck Norris wasn’t just a movie tough guy. He was a legitimate, seven-time world champion who earned his status the hard way — through discipline, competition, and respect for the craft. And what made him different? Humility. He never chased fantasy matchups. Never claimed he could beat heavyweight boxers. Never tried to steal shine. While others argued about who they could defeat, Norris focused on what he could learn. He openly respected greats like Mike Tyson, recognizing elite skill when he saw it. No ego. No noise. Just a martial artist who understood mastery takes different forms. That mindset carried him beyond competition. When he transitioned into film and television, Norris didn’t just build a career — he helped make martial arts mainstream. Generations were introduced to discipline, honor, and combat sports through his presence on screen. He made toughness look controlled. He made confidence look quiet. He made martial arts look cool. Most legends leave highlights. Few leave reputations. Norris walked away with both. In a fight world full of hype and callouts, his legacy stands on something rarer: credibility without arrogance. Win with class. Compete with respect. Leave the sport better than you found it. That’s why the jokes live on. That’s why the respect never fades. #ChuckNorris #MartialArts #CombatSports #Legends #FightCulture #Respect #MartialArtsMovies #CombatLegacy

articles.title: Did Dana Accidentally Reveal Conor’s Opponent?
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to what may have been an accidental announcement from Dana White—did he just reveal Conor McGregor’s next opponent? After watching the interview closely, Chael believes Dana may have let it slip: Conor McGregor vs. Ian Garry. While it wasn’t framed as an official announcement, the wording—and the context—suggest something more than a simple mistake. Chael breaks down why this fight actually makes sense. Conor has made it clear he wants to compete at 170 pounds and isn’t interested in picking favorable matchups. Meanwhile, Ian Garry has been waiting for a big opportunity after previously accepting a high-risk fight that never materialized. There’s also the added layer of respect between the two fighters, making this a unique and potentially complicated matchup. Was this a true announcement… or a rare slip from Dana White? #UFC #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Did Dana Announce It? 0:25 Conor Wants 170 0:53 The Prates Fight Story 1:14 Why Garry Is a Tough Matchup 1:45 The “Slip” Explained 2:13 Why This Fight Makes Sense 2:45 Conor’s Mindset 3:45 Why Garry Deserves It 4:13 Final Thoughts on the Fight Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: This Advice Could Cost Aspinall Everything
articles.description: Get $10 Off at BRUNT with code CHAEL at https://www.bruntworkwear.com/CHAEL #BruntpodChael Sonnen reacts to Eddie Hearn advising Tom Aspinall not to fight—and calls it one of the worst pieces of advice he’s ever heard. According to Chael, this isn’t about money or contracts—it’s about opportunity. Aspinall has already done the hard part: becoming champion. Walking away now, or refusing to fight over contract disputes, could risk everything he’s worked for. Chael breaks down why telling a fighter not to compete goes against the entire foundation of the sport and explains how rare it is to even reach this level in the first place. He also revisits Aspinall’s own stance from a year ago, when he demanded activity, accountability, and a path to the undisputed title—regardless of opponent. Now, with new management and a different message, Chael questions what changed—and whether this advice could put Aspinall in the same position he once criticized. In the end, Chael makes it clear: in this sport, you either fight… or you don’t. #UFC #TomAspinall #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Eddie Hearn’s Advice 0:37 Aspinall Was Right Before 1:25 How Hard This Path Is 2:38 What Happens If He Doesn’t Fight 3:41 The Contract Argument 5:03 Fight or Don’t Fight 6:00 Aspinall’s Past موقف 6:34 Final Take on the AdviceBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: UFC Made a Matchmaking Mistake
articles.description: Sonnen reacts to the Michael “Venom” Page vs. Sam Patterson fight and explains why the disappointing result wasn’t the fighters’ fault. While many fans criticized the lack of action, Chael points to a deeper issue—this was a matchup between training partners who know each other too well. When fighters share history, instincts change. Instead of looking for openings to attack, they often look for reasons not to engage. Chael breaks down how fighting a teammate creates a unique mental barrier and why even elite fighters can struggle to overcome it. He also defends MVP, reminding fans of the excitement he’s consistently delivered throughout his career and why one slow fight shouldn’t erase that. Finally, Chael connects the situation to a bigger issue in the sport—consistency. If wins matter in one fight, they should matter in all of them. A closer look at matchmaking, fighter psychology, and why this fight never had a real chance to deliver. #UFC #MVP #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Fight Was Boring 0:17 Not MVP or Patterson’s Fault 0:45 The Teammate Problem 1:46 Why They Didn’t Engage 2:10 Counter-Striker Issue 3:08 MVP’s Track Record 3:38 Teammates Must Fight 4:06 The Mental Barrier 4:40 Consistency in Judging 5:25 Why MVP Shouldn’t Be Punished Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Jon Jones Didn’t Take Less, He Took Nothing
articles.description: Chael Sonnen breaks down the real reason Jon Jones is not fighting on the UFC’s White House card—and why this situation has nothing to do with taking less money. According to Chael, Jon Jones didn’t choose a smaller payday—he chose no payday at all. After reports surfaced that Jones was willing to fight Alex Pereira for $15 million, Chael explains why that number may have ultimately taken him out of the running entirely. This isn’t just about Jon Jones. It’s about how the UFC operates. Chael dives into the reality of contracts, leverage, and why the promotion has historically shown it can move forward without any single fighter—even one of the greatest of all time. He also compares Jones’ situation to Alex Pereira, who reportedly accepted a new deal and said yes to the opportunity, reinforcing a core message: the right fighter always shows up. In the end, Chael lays out the harsh truth—if you don’t say yes when the call comes, the UFC moves on. #UFC #JonJones #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Jon Jones Took Nothing 0:17 The $15 Million Problem 1:03 Jon’s Message Explained 2:02 Contract Confusion 2:56 The UFC Didn’t Need Him 4:07 The Real UFC Message 5:25 It’s Always Yes or No 6:08 Pereira Comparison 7:05 The Right Guy Shows Up 8:04 Pricing Yourself Out 9:00 Final Verdict on Jon Jones Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Best NCAA Finals Ever… But They Missed This
articles.description: Chael Sonnen breaks down what he believes was one of the greatest NCAA Wrestling Championship finals of all time—but not without controversy. From Jax Forster being elevated to the main event to Sergio Vega making history as an undefeated true freshman national champion, the night delivered unforgettable moments. But Chael points out a hidden consequence most fans missed: changing the match order may have cost another athlete a place in the record books. He also dives into one of the most fascinating production changes in recent memory—the introduction of a mysterious “black phone” used during replay reviews. According to Chael, it didn’t just resolve disputes—it completely eliminated controversy, raising bigger questions about authority and presentation in sports. Finally, Chael reacts to the NCAA introducing championship belts, praising the concept but criticizing the execution in a way only he can. A breakdown of wrestling, production, and the subtle decisions that shape history. #NCAAWrestling #ChaelSonnen #Wrestling 0:00 Best Finals Ever 0:23 Jax Main Event Decision 1:55 Vega Makes History 2:24 Did Jax Get Robbed? 4:26 The Review System Problem 5:46 The Black Phone 8:06 The Belt Controversy Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.description: Michael Bisping gives his instant reaction to UFC London as Movsar Evloev takes on Lerone Murphy in a high-stakes featherweight main event at the O2 Arena. Evloev keeps his undefeated record intact with a hard-fought majority decision despite a point deduction, handing Murphy the first loss of his career and pushing himself firmly into title contention. Bisping breaks down the key moments from the main event, including Murphy’s fast start, Evloev’s relentless wrestling down the stretch, and the debate around the scorecards—along with what this means for the featherweight division moving forward. articles.title: The Round Count Problem in Boxing #ufc
articles.description: Has boxing been stretched too long? There’s a growing argument that more rounds don’t make better fights — they make slower ones. Fighters pace themselves. They conserve energy. They manage risk. But shorten the clock? Everything changes. Less time means urgency. Urgency means output. Output means action. That’s why matchups featuring names like Jake Paul and Michael Page (MVP) tend to feel fast, aggressive, and fan-friendly. Eight rounds forces engagement. Six rounds could be even wilder. Same athletes. Same skills. Same power. Just less runway to coast. When fighters know there’s limited time, they don’t bank energy for later — they spend it now. The total punches thrown often stay similar, but the intensity spikes because there’s no luxury to stall. Longer fights create strategy-heavy pacing. Shorter fights create urgency-heavy action. It’s a tradeoff between chess and chaos. And for casual fans? Chaos wins. This isn’t about discrediting endurance or elite tactics. It’s about entertainment value and how fight structure shapes behavior inside the ring. More rounds can mean: • Slower starts • Energy management • Lower early risk Fewer rounds can mean: • Faster exchanges • Higher output • Immediate pressure You choose the format. But one version consistently delivers more action per minute. And in the attention economy, action is king. #Boxing #JakePaul #MVP #FightNight #CombatSports #BoxingDebate #Prizefighting #FightFans #Knockouts

articles.title: Volk Questions the BMF Legacy #ufc
articles.description: Alexander Volkanovski didn’t sugarcoat it: “Strip the BMF belt. Strip it.” Not a shot at the fighters. Not personal. A statement about expectations vs reality. The BMF title is supposed to guarantee chaos. Violence. A fight people never forget. But when a matchup featuring legends like Max Holloway and Charles Oliveira doesn’t deliver that kind of spectacle… Volk says the belt itself should be reconsidered. That’s a bold stance — but it opens a bigger conversation: What is the BMF belt supposed to represent? A style? A mindset? Or just a marketing label? Because if it’s about action and danger, then every BMF fight carries a promise to fans. And when the fight plays out more tactical than violent, some believe the belt loses meaning. Volk’s solution? Vacate it. Reset it. Let it be earned again. Not everyone will agree. Some fans say the fighters showed elite skill. Others say entertainment is part of the job. Meanwhile, everyone wants different outcomes: Rules changed. Belts reassigned. Rankings adjusted. Nobody gets everything they want. But the debate itself? Compelling. Because it forces the sport to answer a hard question: Is legacy built on names — or on moments? You decide. #UFC #MMA #Volkanovski #MaxHolloway #CharlesOliveira #BMF #FightTalk #CombatSports #MMADebate

articles.title: Ask Uncle Chael: Bo Nickal, Jon Jones, NCAA Wrestling & UFC London
articles.description: It’s time for Ask Uncle Chael, and this one goes everywhere. Bo Nickal asks me a wrestling question I actually love. We get into par terre, gut wrenches, and whether I could turn Bo in a best-of-five. Then we hit the NCAA Championships: * my favorite storyline at 133 * why Jax Forrest is so fascinating * the pressure on PJ Duke * why heavyweight isn’t boring anymore * and my hottest take of all… Nobody actually cares about the team race. We also get into: * Jon Jones in his prime * Chuck Norris and mortality * Jake Paul, Netflix, and whether fun MMA can really compete with the UFC * Scottie Pippen’s motivational posts * UFC London and an ugly weight miss * and the real reason some elite wrestlers dominate in MMA while others never translate 0:00 – Ask Uncle Chael Begins 0:08 – Bo Nickal’s Question: Me vs You in Par Terre 0:40 – Could I Turn Bo Nickal? 1:13 – Why My Best Claim Is the Gut Wrench 1:35 – Favorite NCAA Storyline This Year 2:00 – Why 133 Pounds Is the Best Story in Wrestling 2:43 – The Case for Jax Forrest 3:20 – David Taylor’s Role in the Story 3:51 – How Jax Forrest Got to Stillwater So Fast 4:11 – The Chaos at 133 Gets Even Better 5:04 – Other NCAA Storylines I’m Watching 6:13 – My Hottest Take: Nobody Cares About the Team Race 7:13 – Why the Team Race Is Make-Believe 8:28 – What’s Been Most Interesting at NCAAs? 9:00 – Why the NCAA Tournament Always Falls Apart 9:48 – The Real Advantage at Nationals 10:35 – Me vs Jon Jones in Our Prime11:53 – The Truth About Jon Jones 12:18 – Does Chuck Norris Dying Make Me Think About Mortality? articles.title: Arman Tsarukyan Admits… “I Planned It All Along”
articles.description: Chael just spent the day in Big Bear with Arman Tsarukyan—and what he told me caught me completely off guard. After his match with Giorgio turned into a full brawl, I assumed something happened. Something was said. Something triggered it. But when I asked him? He said: “No. He did nothing.” Then he explained it— It was planned. Tsarukyan said he waited for the final whistle so he wouldn’t get disqualified…then attacked. No excuses. No blame. Just honesty. We also get into: * Training in Big Bear with Chimaev * Competing in MMA, wrestling, and jiu-jitsu at the same time * And the strange debate over whether the match even officially ended This is one of the most honest—and most surprising—conversations I’ve had in a while. 0:00 – Inside Big Bear Training Camp with Tsarukyan 0:19 – The History of Big Bear (De La Hoya & Tito Ortiz) 0:28 – Armenians vs Dagestanis… One House Divided 1:00 – Chimaev Training in Big Bear 1:35 – What “#1 Contender” Actually Means 2:00 – Tsarukyan Competing in THREE Sports 2:43 – The Truth About His Training Split 3:32 – The Match That Turned Into a Brawl 4:09 – What REALLY Happened After the Whistle 4:39 – Tsarukyan’s Shocking Honesty 5:18 – “This Was My Plan All Along” 6:20 – The Rule Debate: Was the Match Even Official? 7:01 – Giorgio’s Argument Changes Everything 8:08 – Khabib, Conor & The Belt ComparisonBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
